structured.description: A `Shader` class that loads and manages shaders for a 3D graphics pipeline. The class provides methods to bind and unbind shaders, as well as uniform and matrix uniform functions. Additionally, it includes methods to validate and link shaders. The code uses the `GL20` package to interact with the OpenGL API.

items.description: has a constructor that takes an integer program ID and provides methods to bind the program, get the program ID, and set uniform values for floating-point and matrix4f data types. Additionally, there are methods to load vertex and fragment shaders and validate the program.
items.name: Shader

items.description: glues a program to a specific GL context, enabling its use for rendering operations.
items.name: bind

items.description: retrieves the value of the `program` field of the current instance.

returns.description: an integer value representing the program.

items.name: getProgram

items.description: takes a string name and an array of float values, and uses the `glUniformf()` method to set the corresponding uniform value(s) in the current GL context. The length of the input array determines which `glUniformf()` method is called, with longer arrays using more parameters.

params.name: name
params.description: name of the uniform location in the GL context, which is used to determine the appropriate OpenGL function to call for the specified number of float values.

usage.description: This will send the value 1 to the uniform variable "name" in the shader.

items.name: uniformf

items.description: sets a 4x4 uniform matrix value using the `glUniformMatrix4` method from the OpenGL API. The matrix data is passed as a parameter and flipped before being stored in the GPU's memory location.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location where the matrix will be stored in the GPU memory.

params.name: mat
params.description:
4x4 matrix to be uniformed and is passed as an instance of the `Matrix4f` class.

* `getLocation(name)` is used to retrieve the location of a uniform buffer in the current program state.
* `GL20.glUniformMatrix4()` is a method that sets a 4x4 matrix as a uniform variable in the currently active program.
* `false` indicates that the matrix should be stored in row-major order, which is the default for GL matrices.
* `Util.createFlippedBuffer(mat.getLinearData())` creates a new buffer containing the matrix data in row-major order, and returns it as a flipped copy of the original matrix.

usage.description: In this example, the `unifromMat4` method is being used to set the value of a uniform named "myUniform" in a shader program. The first parameter passed into the method is the name of the uniform as a string, and the second parameter is an instance of the Matrix4f class. This method will automatically create a flipped buffer using the `Util.createFlippedBuffer` method and pass it to the `GL20.glUniformMatrix4` function, which will then update the value of the uniform in the shader program with the specified matrix data.

items.name: unifromMat4

items.description: retrieves the location of a uniform named `name` within a program's shader, using the `GL20` class to interact with the GPU.

params.name: name
params.description: 0-based index of the uniform location to retrieve in the program's uniform buffer objects.

returns.description: an integer representing the location of a uniform in a graphics program.

items.name: getLoaction

items.description: creates a shader program by loading vertex and fragment shaders from files, validating the program, and returning a new shader object representing the program.

params.name: filename
params.description: file name of the shader to be loaded.

returns.description:
a new `Shader` object representing a shader program created by combining a vertex shader and a fragment shader.

* The returned value is an instance of the `Shader` class, which represents a shader program in the OpenGL context.
* The `Shader` object contains information about the shader program, including its handle (program), which can be used to access and manipulate the program's resources.
* The `Shader` object also stores the handles of the vertex and fragment shaders that make up the program, allowing for easy manipulation and modification of these components.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: creates a new shader program and attaches it to a program, loading the given source code as a shader module.

params.name: target
params.description: type of shader to create, with possible values of 0 for vertex shader, 1 for fragment shader, and 128 for geometry shader.

params.name: src
params.description: source code of the shader to be compiled.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D graphics program that the loaded shader will be attached to and used by.

items.name: loadShader

items.description: validates a program by linking and validating it using the `glLinkProgram` and `glValidateProgram` functions, respectively. If any errors occur during validation, the function prints the error message to `System.err` and exits with a non-zero status code.

params.name: program
params.description: 3D program to be validated and linked with the OpenGL driver.

items.name: validateProgram

items.description: reads the contents of a given file as a string, handling potential IOExceptions.

params.name: file
params.description: file whose contents are to be read and returned as a string by the `getText()` function.

returns.description: a string containing the contents of the specified file.

items.name: getText

structured.description: a class `Model` that represents a 3D model in an OpenGL context. It provides a constructor to set the vertex array object (VAO) and the number of vertices, and a method `draw()` to render the model. The `enableAttribs()` and `disableAttribs()` methods enable and disable specific vertex attributes, respectively. The `getVAO()` and `getSize()` methods provide accessors for the VAO and size, respectively. Additionally, the code provides a factory method `load()` to load a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices.

items.description: is used to handle vertex and index buffer data for rendering 3D models in OpenGL. It provides methods for binding and unbinding Vertex Array Objects (VAOs) and Element Array Buffers (EABs), as well as storing vertex and index data and enabling/disabling vertex attributtes. The class also provides a way to load pre-defined model data from an array of vertices and indices.
items.name: Model

items.description: binds a vertex array object, enables vertex attributes for position and texture coord, draws triangles using `glDrawElements`, disables vertex attributes, and unbinds the vertex array object.
items.name: draw

items.description: enables two vertex attributes in a GL context using `glEnableVertexAttribArray`.
items.name: enableAttribs

items.description: disables both vertex attribute arrays for a specific GL context.
items.name: disableAttribs

items.description: returns the value of the variable `vao`.

returns.description: an integer value representing the VAO.

usage.code:
Model model = Model.load(vertices, indices);
model.getVAO(); // returns the VAO value

items.name: getVAO

items.description: retrieves the value of a field named `size`.
items.name: getSize

items.description: loads a 3D model from an array of vertices and an array of indices. It creates a Vertex Array Object (VAO), stores the indices, and then binds and unbinds the VAO. Finally, it returns a `Model` object representing the loaded model.

params.name: vertices
params.description:
3D geometric data of the model to be loaded, which is stored in an array of floating-point values.

* `vertices`: An array of floating-point values representing the 3D vertices of a model.
* `indices`: An array of integers representing the indices of the vertices in the model.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D model's vertex indices, which are used to bind the vertices of the model to its corresponding geometry in the graphics pipeline.

* `indices`: An `int[]` array containing the indices of the vertices in the model.
* `v_count`: The number of vertices in the model, which can be calculated by summing up the lengths of all the `indices` arrays.

returns.description:
a `Model` object representing the loaded 3D model.

* `Model`: This is the type of the output, which is an instance of the `Model` class.
* `vao`: This is an integer variable representing the VAO (Vertex Array Object) handle created by the function.
* `v_count`: This is an integer variable representing the number of vertices in the model.

items.name: load

items.description: generates a new vertex array object (Vao) and binds it to the current context, allowing for its use in rendering graphics.

returns.description: an integer value representing the unique vertex array object (VAO) created.

items.name: createVAO

items.description: stores floating-point array data into a buffer object and sets vertex attributes for rendering.

params.name: attrib
params.description: 3D vertex attribute index that stores the data for the current buffer in the GPU's memory.

params.name: data
params.description:
2D array of floating-point values that will be stored in the vertex buffer object (VBO).

* `data`: An array of 4 float values representing vertex data.
* `attrib`: An integer representing the attribute index to which the vertex data will be mapped. There are two attribute indices in total, assigned using the `GL20.glVertexAttribPointer()` method.
* `vbo`: A buffer object handle generated by `GL15.glGenBuffers()`. The buffer is bound using `GL15.glBindBuffer(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, vbo)` and data is transferred to it using `GL15.glBufferData(GL15.GL_ARRAY_BUFFER, Util.createFlippedBuffer(data), GL15.GL_STATIC_DRAW)`.

items.name: storeData

items.description: genrates a new buffer object and binds it to an element array buffer, then stores the provided integer array data in the buffer using the `glBufferData` method.

params.name: indices
params.description:
3D indices of the vertices of a geometric shape, which are stored in a buffer for later use in rendering.

* `indices` is an integer array representing a buffer containing indices for an element array buffer.
* The length of the array is specified by the size of the `indices` array, which is a variable passed as a parameter to the function.
* Each element in the `indices` array represents an index into a buffer for an element array, with each index being an integer value between 0 and the total number of elements in the buffer - 1, inclusive.

items.name: storeIndices

items.description: binds a vertex array object (VAO) using the `GL30.glBindVertexArray()` method, setting it to null.
items.name: unbindVAO
